evals.name: user-profile-feature-slice
evals.prompt: We're adding a User Profile screen to our Compose Multiplatform app. Need view/edit modes — user sees their name/email/avatar, can tap edit, change name and avatar, save. Errors from API should show inline. Scaffold the feature folder using our compose-arch layering — Screen, View, Component, UseCase, Repository. Show file tree and minimal code per file (signatures + 1-2 lines body). Don't bother with Android/iOS bootstrapping or Gradle.
evals.expected_output: Feature module skeleton with explicit Screen/View/Component/UseCase/Repository layering. Component holds state and handles intents; View is stateless composable; UseCase is interface + impl; Repository is interface + impl.
assertions: Output names all five layers explicitly: Screen, View, Component, UseCase, Repository
Component holds state (StateFlow / MutableStateFlow or equivalent observable) and exposes intent functions
View is stateless @Composable receiving state + callbacks (no remember of business state)
UseCase is interface + impl, returns domain types (or Result/typed error)
Repository is interface + impl, repository handles network/persistence
Errors from API surface back to UI via state (sealed error type or error string in state)
File tree is explicit (directory + filenames listed)
No HTTP/JSON parsing inside @Composable or Component

evals.name: refactor-god-screen
evals.prompt: I have a CartScreen.kt that does everything in one composable: keeps mutable state with `remember { mutableStateOf(...) }`, calls our `OkHttpClient.newCall(...)` directly inside a `LaunchedEffect`, parses JSON inline, and writes results to a `cartItems` list. It's about 280 lines. We use Compose Multiplatform and follow your Screen/View/Component/UseCase/Repository layering. Walk me through how to break this apart — what goes where, what types/interfaces I'd introduce, and how state and side-effects flow across layers. Don't write the full refactor, just the design and migration plan with short code stubs for each layer.
evals.expected_output: Migration plan that names the layers, moves HTTP call into Repository, business logic into UseCase, state into Component, UI into View, with explicit one-direction data flow.
assertions: Migration plan addresses each layer separately (Repository, UseCase, Component, View, Screen)
OkHttp call moved into Repository implementation
Mutable state moved out of @Composable into Component
Side-effect (network) lives in Repository or invoked via UseCase, not in LaunchedEffect inside View
JSON parsing moved out of UI layer (Repository or serialization layer)
Plan shows one-direction data flow: View -> Component (intents) -> UseCase -> Repository
Includes code stubs/signatures per layer (not just prose)
